verses.latin_vulgate: Psalmus David. Adversus Goliath.   Benedictus Dominus Deus meus,  qui docet manus meas ad prælium,  et digitos meos ad bellum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְדָוִ֨ד ׀ בָּ֘ר֤וּךְ יְהֹוָ֨ה ׀ צוּרִ֗י הַֽמְלַמֵּ֣ד יָדַ֣י לַקְרָ֑ב אֶ֝צְבְּעוֹתַ֗י לַמִּלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Blessed be the Lord my God, who teacheth my hands to fight, and my fingers to war.
verses.finnish_biblia: Davidin Psalmi. Kiitetty olkoon Herra, minun turvani, joka käteni opettaa sotimaan, ja sormeni tappelemaan,
verses.king_james: Blessed be the Lord my strength, which teacheth my hands to war, and my fingers to fight:

verses.latin_vulgate: Misericordia mea et refugium meum ;  susceptor meus et liberator meus ;  protector meus, et in ipso speravi,  qui subdit populum meum sub me.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חַסְדִּ֥י וּמְצוּדָתִי֮ מִשְׂגַּבִּ֢י וּֽמְפַלְטִ֫י־לִ֥י מָ֭גִנִּי וּב֣וֹ חָסִ֑יתִי הָרוֹדֵ֖ד עַמִּ֣י תַחְתָּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: My mercy, and my refuge: my support, and my deliverer: My protector, and I have hoped in him: who subdueth my people under me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minun laupiuteni ja minun linnani, minun varjelukseni ja minun vapahtajani, minun kilpeni, johon minä uskallan, joka minun kansani minun alleni vaatii.
verses.king_james: My goodness, and my fortress; my high tower, and my deliverer; my shield, and he in whom I trust; who subdueth my people under me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, quid est homo, quia innotuisti ei ?  aut filius hominis, quia reputas eum ?
verses.douay_rheims: Lord, what is man, that thou art made known to him? or the son of man, that thou makest account of him?
verses.finnish_biblia: Herra, mikä on ihminen, ettäs häntä korjaat? eli ihmisen poika, ettäs hänestä otat vaarin?
verses.king_james: Lord, what is man, that thou takest knowledge of him! or the son of man, that thou makest account of him!

verses.latin_vulgate: Homo vanitati similis factus est ;  dies ejus sicut umbra prætereunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָ֭דָם לַהֶ֣בֶל דָּמָ֑ה יָ֝מָ֗יו כְּצֵ֣ל עוֹבֵֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Man is like to vanity: his days pass away like a shadow.
verses.finnish_biblia: On sittekin ihminen tyhjän verta: hänen aikansa katoo niinkuin varjo.
verses.king_james: Man is like to vanity: his days are as a shadow that passeth away.

verses.latin_vulgate: Domine, inclina cælos tuos, et descende ;  tange montes, et fumigabunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְ֭הֹוָה הַט־שָׁמֶ֣יךָ וְתֵרֵ֑ד גַּ֖ע בֶּהָרִ֣ים וְֽיֶעֱשָֽׁנוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Lord, bow down thy heavens and descend: touch the mountains, and they shall smoke.
verses.finnish_biblia: Herra, kallista sinun taivaas, ja astu alas: rupee vuoriin, että he suitsisivat.
verses.king_james: Bow thy heavens, O Lord, and come down: touch the mountains, and they shall smoke.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fulgura coruscationem, et dissipabis eos ;  emitte sagittas tuas, et conturbabis eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּר֣וֹק בָּ֭רָק וּתְפִיצֵ֑ם שְׁלַ֥ח חִ֝צֶּ֗יךָ וּתְהֻמֵּֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Send forth lightning, and thou shalt scatter them: shoot out thy arrows, and thou shalt trouble them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Anna leimaukset iskeä, ja hajoita heitä: ammu nuolias ja kauhistuta heitä.
verses.king_james: Cast forth lightning, and scatter them: shoot out thine arrows, and destroy them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Emitte manum tuam de alto : eripe me,  et libera me de aquis multis,  de manu filiorum alienorum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁלַ֥ח יָדֶ֗יךָ מִ֫מָּר֥וֹם פְּצֵ֣נִי וְ֭הַצִּילֵנִי מִמַּ֣יִם רַבִּ֑ים מִ֝יַּ֗ד בְּנֵ֣י נֵכָֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Put forth thy hand from on high, take me out, and deliver me from many waters: from the hand of strange children:
verses.finnish_biblia: Lähetä kätes ylhäältä, ja kirvota minua, ja pelasta minua suurista vesistä ja muukalaisten lasten käsistä,
verses.king_james: Send thine hand from above; rid me, and deliver me out of great waters, from the hand of strange children;

verses.latin_vulgate: quorum os locutum est vanitatem,  et dextera eorum dextera iniquitatis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֣ר פִּ֭יהֶם דִּבֶּר־שָׁ֑וְא וִ֝ימִינָ֗ם יְמִ֣ין שָֽׁקֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Whose mouth hath spoken vanity: and their right hand is the right hand of iniquity.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joiden suu puhuu valhetta, ja heidän oikia kätensä on petollinen oikia käsi.
verses.king_james: Whose mouth speaketh vanity, and their right hand is a right hand of falsehood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Deus, canticum novum cantabo tibi ;  in psalterio decachordo psallam tibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱֽלֹהִ֗ים שִׁ֣יר חָ֭דָשׁ אָשִׁ֣ירָה לָּ֑ךְ בְּנֵ֥בֶל עָ֝שׂ֗וֹר אֲזַמְּרָה־לָּֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: To thee, O God, I will sing a new canticle: on the psaltery and an instrument of ten strings I will sing praises to thee.
verses.finnish_biblia: Jumala, minä veisaan sinulle uuden virren: minä soitan sinulle kymmenkielisellä psaltarilla,
verses.king_james: I will sing a new song unto thee, O God: upon a psaltery and an instrument of ten strings will I sing praises unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui das salutem regibus,  qui redemisti David servum tuum de gladio maligno,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַנּוֹתֵ֥ן תְּשׁוּעָ֗ה לַמְּלָ֫כִ֥ים הַ֭פּוֹצֶה אֶת־דָּוִ֥ד עַבְדּ֗וֹ מֵחֶ֥רֶב רָעָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Who givest salvation to kings: who hast redeemed thy servant David from the malicious sword:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä joka kuninkaille voiton annat, ja palvelias Davidin vapahdat murhamiekasta.
verses.king_james: It is he that giveth salvation unto kings: who delivereth David his servant from the hurtful sword.

verses.latin_vulgate: eripe me, et erue me de manu filiorum alienorum,  quorum os locutum est vanitatem,  et dextera eorum dextera iniquitatis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פְּצֵ֥נִי וְהַצִּילֵנִי֮ מִיַּ֢ד בְּֽנֵי־נֵ֫כָ֥ר אֲשֶׁ֣ר פִּ֭יהֶם דִּבֶּר־שָׁ֑וְא וִ֝ימִינָ֗ם יְמִ֣ין שָֽׁקֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Deliver me, And rescue me out of the hand of strange children; whose mouth hath spoken vanity: and their right hand is the right hand of iniquity:
verses.finnish_biblia: Päästä myös minua ja pelasta minua muukalaisten kädestä, joiden suu puhuu valhetta, ja heidän oikia kätensä on petollinen oikia käsi,
verses.king_james: Rid me, and deliver me from the hand of strange children, whose mouth speaketh vanity, and their right hand is a right hand of falsehood:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quorum filii sicut novellæ plantationes in juventute sua ;  filiæ eorum compositæ,  circumornatæ ut similitudo templi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֤ר בָּנֵ֨ינוּ ׀ כִּנְטִעִים֮ מְגֻדָּלִ֢ים בִּֽנְעוּרֵ֫יהֶ֥ם בְּנוֹתֵ֥ינוּ כְזָוִיֹּ֑ת מְ֝חֻטָּב֗וֹת תַּבְנִ֥ית הֵיכָֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Whose sons are as new plants in their youth: Their daughters decked out, adorned round about after the similitude of a temple:
verses.finnish_biblia: Että meidän pojat kasvaisivat nuoruudessansa niinkuin vesat, ja meidän tyttäret, niinkuin templin kaunistetut seinät.
verses.king_james: That our sons may be as plants grown up in their youth; that our daughters may be as corner stones, polished after the similitude of a palace:

verses.latin_vulgate: Promptuaria eorum plena, eructantia ex hoc in illud ;  oves eorum fœtosæ, abundantes in egressibus suis ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מְזָוֵ֣ינוּ מְלֵאִים֮ מְפִיקִ֥ים מִזַּ֗ן אֶ֫ל־זַ֥ן צֹאונֵ֣נוּ מַ֭אֲלִיפוֹת מְרֻבָּב֗וֹת בְּחוּצוֹתֵֽינוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Their storehouses full, flowing out of this into that. Their sheep fruitful in young, abounding in their goings forth:
verses.finnish_biblia: Meidän aittamme olkoon täynnä, jotka runsaat elatukset antaisivat toinen toisensa perästä, että meidän lampaamme poikisivat tuhannen, ja sata tuhatta, kylissämme;
verses.king_james: That our garners may be full, affording all manner of store: that our sheep may bring forth thousands and ten thousands in our streets:

verses.latin_vulgate: boves eorum crassæ.  Non est ruina maceriæ, neque transitus,  neque clamor in plateis eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַלּוּפֵ֗ינוּ מְֽסֻבָּ֫לִ֥ים אֵֽין־פֶּ֭רֶץ וְאֵ֣ין יוֹצֵ֑את וְאֵ֥ין צְ֝וָחָ֗ה בִּרְחֹבֹתֵֽינוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Their oxen fat. There is no breach of wall, nor passage, nor crying out in their streets.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että härkämme olisivat vahvat työhön; ettei yhtään vahinkoa, eikä valitusta eli kannetta olisi kaduillamme.
verses.king_james: That our oxen may be strong to labour; that there be no breaking in, nor going out; that there be no complaining in our streets.

verses.latin_vulgate: Beatum dixerunt populum cui hæc sunt ;  beatus populus cujus Dominus Deus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַשְׁרֵ֣י הָ֭עָם שֶׁכָּ֣כָה לּ֑וֹ אַֽשְׁרֵ֥י הָ֝עָ֗ם שֱׁיְהֹוָ֥ה אֱלֹהָֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: They have called the people happy, that hath these things: but happy is that people whose God is the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Autuas on se kansa, jolle niin käy; vaan autuas on se kansa, jonka Jumalana Herra on.
verses.king_james: Happy is that people, that is in such a case: yea, happy is that people, whose God is the Lord.
